description_long: This is a large commercial passenger airplane with a streamlined fuselage painted in a light blue and white color scheme, featuring the KLM logo on the tail fin. The aircraft has a wide-body design with a pronounced hump on the upper forward fuselage, indicating a two-deck configuration typical of long-haul jets. It is equipped with four underwing turbofan engines, two on each wing, and the wings are swept back with slight upward-angled winglets at the tips. The tail section consists of a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, all painted in matching blue and white. The landing gear is not visible in the images, suggesting it is retracted, and the cockpit is located at the very front of the upper fuselage, with multiple windows for pilot visibility. The overall design is optimized for high-capacity, long-distance air travel.
description: A large, blue and white commercial passenger airplane with four underwing engines, swept-back wings with winglets, and a distinctive two-deck forward fuselage.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full wingspan, four engines, and the blue upper fuselage with white wings and tail.
description_view_1: Left side profile highlighting the KLM logo on the tail, the cockpit windows, and the two-deck forward fuselage.
description_view_2: Bottom-up view displaying the underwing engines, the landing gear doors, and the full span of the wings and tail.
description_view_3: Right side profile similar to view 1, with the KLM logo and the distinctive upper deck visible.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, swept-back wings with upward-angled winglets at the tips and moderate dihedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Four underwing turbofan engines, two per wing.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle landing gear, with main gear under the wings and a nose gear (not visible, inferred from typical configuration).
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed multi-seat cockpit with multiple windows at the front of the upper fuselage.
